# Argument Map

## Claim 1
Digital markets rely on proxy signals.

## Claim 2
Proxy signals determine allocation: attention, trust, money, legitimacy.

## Claim 3
Because proxy signals create value, actors have incentives to distort them.

## Claim 4
Manufactured traction is the cross-domain pattern of distorting those signals.

## Claim 5
This appears in social media, commerce, app stores, ads, music, search, and startups.

## Claim 6
The ethical problem is systemic: corrupted proof causes corrupted allocation.

## Conclusion
When proof becomes synthetic, markets lose the ability to reward real value.
